headline: Nvidia exec confirms compute costs now dwarf employee spending
body: An Nvidia executive publicly stated that infrastructure costs for running large language models have surpassed the expense of their workforce, signaling a major shift in AI economics. This threshold matters critically for healthcare organizations evaluating clinical AI deployments, as the compute burden directly impacts ROI calculations for EHR-integrated applications and real-time diagnostic tools. Healthcare systems planning AI infrastructure must now budget for sustained, high-cost compute resources rather than treating them as secondary line items. Vendors offering healthcare AI solutions will face increasing pressure to demonstrate efficiency gains that justify these elevated operational costs, particularly for value-based care models where margin compression is already acute.
